define i32 @test(i1 %c) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i32 @test
; CHECK-SAME: (i1 [[C:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SEL:%.*]] = select i1 [[C]], i32 33, i32 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SUB_I:%.*]] = add nsw i32 [[SEL]], -68
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SHL_I:%.*]] = shl nuw nsw i32 1, [[SUB_I]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ADD:%.*]] = add nsw i32 [[SHL_I]], [[SEL]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SEXT:%.*]] = shl i32 [[ADD]], 24
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CONV2:%.*]] = ashr exact i32 [[SEXT]], 24
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[INVARIANT_OP:%.*]] = sub nsw i32 7, [[CONV2]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use(i32 [[INVARIANT_OP]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SEXT_US:%.*]] = shl i32 [[SEL]], 24
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CONV2_US:%.*]] = ashr exact i32 [[SEXT_US]], 24
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[LOOP:%.*]]
; CHECK:       loop:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 true, label [[EXIT:%.*]], label [[LOOP]]
; CHECK:       exit:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[INVARIANT_OP_US:%.*]] = sub nsw i32 7, [[CONV2_US]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[INVARIANT_OP_US]]
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %c, i32 33, i32 0
  %sub.i = add nsw i32 %sel, -68
  %shl.i = shl nuw nsw i32 1, %sub.i
  %add = add nsw i32 %shl.i, %sel
  %sext = shl i32 %add, 24
  %conv2 = ashr exact i32 %sext, 24
  %invariant.op = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2
  call void @use(i32 %invariant.op)
  %sext.us = shl i32 %sel, 24
  %conv2.us = ashr exact i32 %sext.us, 24
  %invariant.op.us = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2.us
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %loop ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %iv.next = add i32 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %iv.next, %invariant.op.us
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %iv.next
}

  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %iv.next, %invariant.op.us
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %iv.next
}

issue.title: SCEVExpander incorrectly reuses poison-generating values
issue.author: shao-hua-li

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Can you please use Compiler + Executor From This instead of Execution Only for godbolt links?

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
I think this is a LoopVectorize or possibly SCEVExpander issue. The min.iters check ends up working on a poison value.

cc @fhahn

comments.author: RKSimon
nikic
comments.body: @RKSimon `a` is a global variable, it is implicitly zero-initialized.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Here is the essentially unreduced IR before vectorization: https://gist.github.com/nikic/da4155719e99b1399437c85b1448c557

Note that the issue only reproduces with `-passes='print<scalar-evolution>,loop-vectorize'`, where the former is used to populate the cache. With empty cache we use `%invariant.op22`, with populated cache we use `%invariant.op`.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
And from the SCEV dump we have:
```
[...]
  %invariant.op = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2
  -->  (7 + (-1 * %0)<nsw>)<nsw> U: [-26,8) S: [-26,8)
[...]
  %invariant.op22 = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2.us
  -->  (7 + (-1 * %0)<nsw>)<nsw> U: [-26,8) S: [-26,8)
```
So these have the same SCEV, but `%invariant.op` is actually more poisonous, so we run into trouble when SCEVExpander tries to reuse it.

We have some code to clear poison flags when reusing values, but it only looks at the last instruction.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Here's a reduction:
```llvm
define i32 @test(i1 %.b) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %.b, i32 33, i32 0
  %sub.i = add nsw i32 %sel, -68
  %shl.i = shl nuw nsw i32 1, %sub.i
  %add = add nsw i32 %shl.i, %sel
  %sext = shl i32 %add, 24
  %conv2 = ashr exact i32 %sext, 24
  %invariant.op = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2
  call void @use(i32 %invariant.op)
  %sext.us = shl i32 %sel, 24
  %conv2.us = ashr exact i32 %sext.us, 24
  %invariant.op.us = sub nsw i32 7, %conv2.us
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i32 [ %iv.next , %loop ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %iv.next = add i32 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %iv.next, %invariant.op.us
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:
  ret i32 %iv.next
}

declare void @use(i32)
```

This one is for `-passes='print<scalar-evolution>,indvars'`, which gives us:
```llvm
define i32 @test(i1 %.b) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %.b, i32 33, i32 0
  %sub.i = add nsw i32 %sel, -68
  %shl.i = shl nuw nsw i32 1, %sub.i
  %add = add nsw i32 %shl.i, %sel
  %sext = shl i32 %add, 24
  %conv2 = ashr exact i32 %sext, 24
  %invariant.op = sub i32 7, %conv2
  call void @use(i32 %invariant.op)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  br i1 true, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %invariant.op
}
```
While we should be using `%invariant.op.us` (or not reusing).

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: This seems like a pretty tricky issue to me. My best idea right now is that when we reuse an existing value, we need to extract all the possible poison-contributors to the SCEV expression and check whether the IR value can have any poison contributors outside that set.

comments.author: shao-hua-li
